---
title: "Home"
title-block-style: none
pagetitle: "Home | Mesfin Genie"
description: "Dr Mesfin Genie, Continuing Senior Lecturer in Health Economics at The University of Newcastle."
page-layout: full
image: /assets/img/og-card.png
---

```{=html}
<a class="skip-link" href="#main">Skip to main content</a>
<main id="main">
<section class="hero"><div class="page-shell hero-grid"><div><p class="eyebrow">Health economics · Behavioural economics · Choice modelling</p><h1>Dr Mesfin Genie</h1><p class="hero-role">Continuing Senior Lecturer in Health Economics</p><p class="hero-inst">Newcastle Business School, The University of Newcastle, Australia</p><p class="lead">I study how patients and the public value healthcare, process information and respond to policy, using health economics, behavioural economics, discrete choice experiments, eye tracking and decision-support tools.</p><div class="button-row"><a class="button-primary" href="/research/">Explore research</a><a class="button-secondary" href="/publications/">View publications</a><a class="button-secondary" href="/cv/mesfin-genie-cv.pdf" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Download CV</a></div><a class="button-text" href="/supervision/">Collaboration and supervision →</a><div class="profile-links"><a href="https://www.newcastle.edu.au/profile/mesfin-genie" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" aria-label="University profile">Un</a><a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=v2SXM_kAAAAJ&hl=en" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" aria-label="Google Scholar">Go</a><a href="https://orcid.org/0000-0002-1744-4666" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" aria-label="ORCID">OR</a><a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/mesfin-genie/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" aria-label="LinkedIn">Li</a><a href="https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Mesfin-Genie" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" aria-label="ResearchGate">Re</a><a href="https://github.com/DrGenie" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" aria-label="GitHub">Gi</a></div></div><div><div class="portrait-card"><img src="/assets/img/mesfin-genie-profile.webp" width="640" height="640" alt="Portrait of Dr Mesfin Genie" loading="eager"></div><div class="status-pill"><span class="status-dot"></span> Open to collaboration</div></div></div></section>
<section class="content-section"><div class="page-shell"><div class="evidence-strip" aria-label="Verified credibility indicators"><div class="evidence-item"><strong>39</strong><span>Peer-reviewed journal articles in the website data</span></div><div class="evidence-item"><strong>2</strong><span>Major funded or commissioned programmes described with verified records</span></div><div class="evidence-item"><strong>2</strong><span>Live decision-support and economic-evaluation tools</span></div><div class="evidence-item"><strong>7</strong><span>Current, completed or recorded research supervisions</span></div></div></div></section>
<section class="content-section"><div class="page-shell"><div class="section-heading"><div><p class="eyebrow">Research themes</p><h2>How evidence becomes better decisions</h2></div><a class="button-text" href="/research/">Research hub →</a></div><div class="grid-3"><article class="research-card"><span class="tag">Preferences</span><h3>Health preferences and behavioural economics</h3><p>Discrete choice experiments, preference heterogeneity, equity and behavioural mechanisms in health decision-making.</p></article><article class="research-card"><span class="tag">Attention</span><h3>Information processing and eye tracking</h3><p>How people read, ignore, weight and trade off information in multi-attribute health choices.</p></article><article class="research-card"><span class="tag">Policy</span><h3>Health measurement and policy evaluation</h3><p>Evidence for vaccine policy, telehealth, preventive care, clinical outcomes and decision-support tools.</p></article></div></div></section>
<section class="content-section"><div class="page-shell"><div class="section-heading"><div><p class="eyebrow">Selected work</p><h2>Recent publications</h2></div><a class="button-text" href="/publications/">All publications →</a></div><div class="grid-3"><article class="pub-card"><div class="meta">npj Vaccines · 2026</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1038/s41541-026-01454-4" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">An Open Repository of COVID-19 Vaccine Mandate Studies with a Worked Scoping Review of Quasi-experimental Evidence</a></h3><p class="authors">Fabio I. Martinenghi; Tushar P. Ojha; Anaïs Thomasson; Bianca Devsam; Sarah Drislane; Stavros Vasiliadis; Chiara Berardi; Aregawi Gebremariam; Arianna Dipalma; Teresa Gavaruzzi; Jeremy K. Ward; Francesco Paolucci; Margie Danchin; <strong>Mesfin Genie</strong>; Katie Attwell; Christopher C. Blyth</p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1038/s41541-026-01454-4" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI ↗</a></p></article>
<article class="pub-card"><div class="meta">Australian and New Zealand Journal of Public Health · 2026</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.anzjph.2026.100496" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Community First or My Body First? Moral Values and Vaccine Mandate Preferences</a></h3><p class="authors"><strong>Mesfin Genie</strong>; Fabio I. Martinenghi; Francesco Paolucci; Jonathan Lake; Katie Attwell; Christopher C. Blyth; Margie Danchin; Teresa Gavaruzzi; Udo Evers</p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.anzjph.2026.100496" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI ↗</a></p></article>
<article class="pub-card"><div class="meta">Health Policy · 2026</div><h3><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.healthpol.2026.105587" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Feeling Lonely? Preferences for Support Programmes to Reduce Loneliness among Older Adults in Australia: A Discrete Choice Experiment</a></h3><p class="authors"><strong>Mesfin G. Genie</strong>; Allison W. Boyes; Francesco Paolucci</p><p><span class="status">Published</span> <a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.healthpol.2026.105587" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">DOI ↗</a></p></article></div></div></section>
<section class="content-section"><div class="page-shell"><div class="section-heading"><div><p class="eyebrow">Tools</p><h2>Research translated into decisions</h2></div><a class="button-text" href="/tools/">All tools →</a></div><div class="grid-3"><article class="tool-card"><div class="meta">Live</div><h3>eMANDEVAL Future</h3><p>Prospective vaccine-mandate policy analysis.</p><a href="/eMANDEVA-DecisionAid-V18/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Launch or view details ↗</a></article><article class="tool-card"><div class="meta">Live</div><h3>SOIL CRC Benefit-Cost Analysis Tool</h3><p>Economic evaluation of farm-practice and soil-management interventions..</p><a href="/farming-bca-tool-v18/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Launch or view details ↗</a></article><article class="tool-card"><div class="meta">Pending source upload</div><h3>STEPS Decision Aid</h3><p>Prioritisation and planning for field epidemiology training scale-up under budget constraints..</p><a href="/STEPS-FETP-DecisionAid-V20/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Launch or view details ↗</a></article></div></div></section>
<section class="content-section"><div class="page-shell grid-2"><div><p class="eyebrow">Talks and media</p><h2 class="page-section-title">Featured webinar</h2><p><strong>Introduction to the Discrete Choice Experiment as an evaluation tool for rare disease activities.</strong> A recorded webinar on discrete choice experiments and evaluation for rare disease activities.</p><a class="button-secondary" href="/talks-media/">Talks and media →</a></div><div class="youtube-shell"><iframe loading="lazy" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/HO2qXJp-BFs" title="Introduction to the Discrete Choice Experiment as an evaluation tool for rare disease activities" allowfullscreen></iframe></div></div></section>
<section class="content-section"><div class="page-shell"><div class="section-heading"><div><p class="eyebrow">Teaching and supervision</p><h2>Behavioural and health economics teaching</h2></div><a class="button-text" href="/teaching/">Teaching portfolio →</a></div><p class="lead">I coordinate ECON3111 Behavioural Economics, ECON2112 Health Economics and Finance, and ECON3011 Introduction to Health Economics and Finance, and supervise research in health economics, choice modelling, telehealth, public-health policy and economic evaluation.</p></div></section>
<section class="content-section"><div class="page-shell"><div class="section-heading"><div><p class="eyebrow">Writing</p><h2>Latest insights</h2></div><a class="button-text" href="/blog/">All posts →</a></div><div class="grid-3"><article class="post-card"><div class="meta">Behavioural economics</div><h3><a href="/blog/posts/why-99-does-not-feel-like-100/">Why 99% does not feel like 100%</a></h3><p>The certainty effect and what it teaches us about risky choices.</p></article><article class="post-card"><div class="meta">Choice modelling</div><h3><a href="/blog/posts/what-a-discrete-choice-experiment-measures/">What a discrete choice experiment actually measures</a></h3><p>A plain guide to attributes, trade-offs and the limits of stated preferences.</p></article><article class="post-card"><div class="meta">Eye tracking</div><h3><a href="/blog/posts/what-eye-tracking-reveals/">What eye tracking reveals about how we choose</a></h3><p>Why attention and information processing matter in preference research.</p></article></div></div></section>
<section class="content-section"><div class="page-shell narrow"><p class="eyebrow">Contact</p><h2 class="page-section-title">Research, collaboration and supervision</h2><p>For research collaboration, seminar invitations, PhD or MPhil supervision enquiries, or decision-support tool discussions, please contact me by email.</p><a class="button-primary" href="mailto:mesfin.genie@newcastle.edu.au">Email Dr Mesfin Genie</a></div></section>
</main>
```
